reasoning: Bangladesh violence levels significantly exceed ACLED baseline of 31.167 fatalities per 30 days. Evidence shows: 10.66 daily murders (320/month), 650+ extrajudicial killings, political violence at 4-year highs with 160 deaths in 13 months, 40% increase in mob violence. October 15, 2025 report confirms violence at elevated levels. Even if ACLED captures only specific categories (political violence, explosions, riots), current levels of 30+ monthly politically-related deaths alone approach baseline. Given momentum and October reports showing continued clashes, probability of exceeding baseline is high (78%) for near date, decreasing slightly over time (75% for Nov-25, 72% for Jan-26) as uncertainty increases.
